summary: Verified 3-layer email-flood fix on /api/auth/forgot-password. All 6 targeted tests pass and full regression 216/218 (2 skipped, +6 new vs 210 baseline) passes with zero new failures. EMAIL_SEND_ENABLED=0 kill-switch prevents any Resend HTTP call, per-email 15-min cooldown blocks repeat sends for the same address, and response body is identical across all 3 paths (enumeration-safe).
critical_code_review_comments: Kill-switch scope: EMAIL_SEND_ENABLED currently gates ONLY forgot-password. mailer.send_voucher_share_email path still calls Resend directly (verified by recent Resend rate-limit errors for voucher-share). If the intent is a global dev/incident kill-switch, gate should live inside mailer.py or every send_* helper. Recommend hoisting the env check into a single Resend client wrapper.

Cooldown row lookup uses email as the shard/index key — no index defined on password_resets.email + created_at. Under load this is a full-collection scan every forgot-password call. Add a compound index {email:1, created_at:-1}.
test_credentials: Admin: test@perkorbit.app / Perk@1234 (present in DB, used for cooldown+kill-switch verification)
seed_data_creation: None — cleaned up all password_resets rows created for test@perkorbit.app before and after each test
context_for_next_testing_agent: Layer 1 slowapi rate-limit is DISABLED in dev (DISABLE_RATE_LIMIT=1). Do not stress-test forgot-password even with kill-switch on. Cleanup password_resets rows for test@perkorbit.app after every test to avoid cooldown pollution. No Resend calls fired for forgot-password during this iteration — verified via log inspection.
